headlines.title: Actor Russell Andrews reveals ALS diagnosis
headlines.description: Actor Russell Andrews, whose credits include “Better Call Saul,” “Grey’s Anatomy” and “Insecure,” said over the weekend that he has been diagnosed with ALS.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Florida surgeon who removed wrong organ says he is ‘forever traumatized’ by patient’s death
headlines.description: Thomas Shaknovsky said in a deposition in November that he believed he was removing 70-year-old William Bryan’s spleen, not his liver.

headlines.title: How to Encourage Mental Health Conversations With Your Teen
headlines.description: Psychologist Dr. Alfiee Breland-Noble, founder of The AAKOMA Project, joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and guest co-host Carson Daly to share tips from her new book called “Rise and Thrive: A Mental Health Workbook for Youth and for the Young Person Inside All of Us.” She shares how to identity if your child is struggling, ways to encourage conversation, and what to do if someone is experiencing a mental crisis.

headlines.title: Tips for Understanding and Managing Your Blood Pressure
headlines.description: Dr. Icilma Fergus, director of cardiovascular disparities at Mount Sinai, joins TODAY to break down the importance of understanding and managing your blood pressure. She shares factors that contribute to high blood pressure, including stress and low daily activity, explains how to check your own blood pressure at home, and highlights preventative measures you can take before your blood pressure gets too high.

headlines.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda Declared Global Emergency
headlines.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda as a “public health emergency of international concern.” There are more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths.

headlines.title: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Arrives in Netherlands to Be Disinfected
headlines.description: The MV Hondius cruise ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak arrived in the Netherlands Monday where it will be disinfected. Meanwhile, a Canadian passenger has now tested positive as the 18 Americans from that ship remain in quarantine. NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.
